author: Muhammad Akbar, Maulvi
title: Ahsan-ul-Bayan fi Ibahat-id-Dukhan
gloss: A pamphlet in which the writer tries to prove that tobacco-smoking is allowed by the Muhammadan law, with quotations from the Quran and traditions.
notes: Pro-tobacco fatwa in the same quarter as the Temperance Society's anti-tobacco series (p25 s174–175) — the smoking question litigated across presses. Dual date: 1328 Hijri / 18th October 1910.

flags.issue: margin serial obscured by pencil X; read as 93 (94 follows)

author: Muhammad Bashir, Maulvi
title: Fazal-i-Ilahi
gloss: A booklet in which the author gives brief biographies of all the saints of the Naqsbandiyah order.
notes: Naqshbandi hagiographic compendium from Simla. Price line not printed with entry.

flags.issue: no price line visible for this entry

author: Muhammad Muazzam, Maulvi
title: Islam ki Dusri Kitab
gloss: Or a second book of Islam teaching the chief doctrines.
notes: '(Designed for educational purposes.)' printed above entry. Graded 'books of Islam' series (s95–98) mirroring the Text-Book Committee reader format — catechism adopting the school-primer architecture; consecutive regs 1398–1401.

author: Muhammad Muazzam, Maulvi
title: Islam ki Tisri Kitab
gloss: Or third book of Islam, treating of ablution.

author: Muhammad Muazzam, Maulvi
title: Islam ki Chauthi Kitab, Hissa-i-Awwal
gloss: Or fourth book of Islam, treating of daily prayers, Part I.

author: Muhammad Muazzam, Maulvi
title: Islam ki Chauthi Kitab, Hissa-i-Duwum
notes: Author dash-continuation of s97; four registrations in eight days completing the series.

author: Qamar-ud-din, Maulvi
title: Qamar-i-Subhani
gloss: A controversial pamphlet in which the writer tries to prove that to say 'Ya Shaikh Abdul Qadir Jilani,' which means 'Heelp me Oh Shaikh Abdul Qadir Jilani,' is allowed by the Muhammadan law.
notes: Istighasa (calling on saints) defense — the Barelvi–Ahl-i-Hadith fault line; catalog misprints 'Heelp me'. With s89, s91, s93: the quarter's cluster of practice-defense pamphlets.

title: Ruidad-i-Mubahsa-i-Patti
gloss: The proceedings of a religious meeting held at Patti, Amritsar district, by the representatives of the Sunni and the Wahabi sects of Muhammadans, to discuss certain disputed points.
notes: Printed proceedings of a live Sunni–'Wahabi' munazara at Patti, 250 free copies published by an artisan (mistri) — oral disputation converted to print record within the quarter; registrar's 'Wahabi' label preserved verbatim.

flags.issue: margin serial obscured by pencil X; read as 100 (follows 99)
